title: React Component Hooks Lifecycle, Batching & Fiber Reconciliation
description: Master component rendering lifecycles, state batching, dependency array matching rules, and custom hook extractions.
learningObjectives: Explain how React groups state updates into single transaction batches.
Formulate setup and cleanup execution cycles for useEffect.
Create and extract a custom hook that handles window resized event listeners.

resources.name: Rules of Hooks: Top Level Executions

quiz.question: What comparison check does React use under the hood to compare dependency arrays?
What is the primary benefit of React's automatic batching?
options: It groups state updates together to trigger fewer component renders.
It automatically binds variables to global local storage.
quiz.answer: It groups state updates together to trigger fewer component renders.

quiz.question: If you do not return a cleanup function in a useEffect that attaches an event listener, what could happen?
options: React will throw a runtime syntax error.
Multiple event listeners will accumulate on every re-render, causing a memory leak.
The component will fail to compile.
quiz.answer: Multiple event listeners will accumulate on every re-render, causing a memory leak.
